## OQS Technical Steering Committee

## liboqs

### PR [#2410](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2410) — Update GOVERNANCE.md and CODEOWNERS

Douglas opened PR [#2410](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2410) updating `GOVERNANCE.md` and `CODEOWNERS` — addressing the pending task Michael flagged previously — which will need a vote from current liboqs maintainers and committers. The PR proposes removing people who are no longer active and adding people who have become more active in various roles, so members were asked to review. 

### HQC update (PRs [#2328](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2328) / [#2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407))

Bruce is continuing to integrate the official HQC repository but is running into CI pipeline failures that are hard to reproduce locally even on matching x86/Linux environments. No specific help is needed for now beyond patience; the group briefly discussed whether GitHub Actions allows attaching a debugger or SSH access.

### New SLH-DSA parameter sets from NIST

Rodrigo flagged that NIST recently announced a new ("reduced") parameterization of SLH-DSA. He reached out to Matthias and the slhdsa-c maintainers, who plan to add the new parameters once NIST releases KATs and test vectors. Rodrigo opened an issue on the slhdsa-c repo ([pq-code-package/slhdsa-c#46](https://github.com/pq-code-package/slhdsa-c/issues/46)) to track this, and will also open a corresponding issue in liboqs. NIST's current plan is apparently to give these a distinct name rather than call them SLH-DSA, so at some point the project will need to discuss how to expose them.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo):** Open a liboqs issue to track eventual inclusion of the new SLH-DSA (reduced) parameter sets, pending upstream slhdsa-c support.

### PR [#2384](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2384) — Fix reading beyond buffer end

Norm confirmed that the related fuzz-harness PR ([#2400](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2400)) has now been merged, which clears the way for #2384. There are outstanding change requests on #2384 — Norm believes his and Douglas's feedback have been addressed, and that #2400 effectively resolves Douglas's earlier concern; Michael's comment remains to be reconciled. Douglas will take one more look and, assuming it looks good, merge.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Douglas):** Review and merge PR [#2384](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2384).

## OQS-OpenSSL provider

### PR [#769](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/769) — Shared library build

Merged. As discussed previously, shared-library build is now available as an opt-in option; the default module-type behavior is preserved. This also allowed closing one of the stale PRs ([#655](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/655)).

### HQC reactivation (issue [#772](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/issues/772))

Rodrigo opened an issue to track re-enabling HQC by default in the provider once the HQC update lands in liboqs 0.16.0.

### PR [#752](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/752) — Preserve property string for KEMs

Norm enabled the provider query function (which had not been wired up) and added a test that sets a property parameter, queries it back from the provider, and compares. He also investigated the behavior around query strings: OpenSSL filters algorithms/providers based on the query string, so a badly-specified query returns nothing — this is expected OpenSSL behavior, and means applications must be precise (or use the "best effort" fetch mode that returns the first match). Rodrigo confirmed this matches expected behavior: property queries parameterize behavior rather than implementation source. Rodrigo will review Norm's changes soon.

Norm is still working through build failures on his branch on Windows and 32-bit Intel/Mac platforms (undefined functions at link time). Rodrigo offered to help look.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo):** Review PR [#752](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/752) and help Norm diagnose the Windows / 32-bit build failures.

## OQS-OpenSSH

Andrew reported he is starting to look at uplifting to the latest OpenSSH version (10.3). As part of that work he plans to look at the open user story for certificate support in the OQS-SSH key generation flow, to see whether it can be addressed alongside the uplift. Still early.

## Language wrappers

Kamal reported no updates on liboqs-js; nothing to raise on the other wrappers.

## Next meetings

Next week (Tuesday, April 28, 2026, 10:00 AM US Eastern) is the OQS Technical Steering Committee meeting, in a slightly different format as Rodrigo begins taking on the co-chair role. Everyone is welcome. The following week returns to a normal status meeting at 12:30 PM US Eastern.
